core_mechanism: a set of coupled oscillators frozen away from equilibrium by a decaying time-dependent friction term, which begin coherent oscillation and store energy as a persistent condensate once the friction drops below their natural frequency (threshold-triggered release of displacement energy)
brief_reason: The abstract's 'misalignment mechanism of a multi-vector condensate through kinetic coupling during inflation' describes fields held displaced from equilibrium by strong Hubble friction that later oscillate coherently as a stored-energy condensate, i.e. coupled displaced oscillators released when friction falls below threshold.
